title: Strait of Hormuz oil tanker traffic blocked after Israeli strikes, Iranian media says
content:
[Oil Tanker]
HeliRy/E+ via Getty Images

Oil tanker transit through the Strait of Hormuz was halted [https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-08/oil-ships-hormuz-transits-blocked-after-lebanon-hit-fars-says] due to Israeli attacks on Lebanon, Iran's semi-official Fars News Agency reported Wednesday, potentially jeopardizing [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4573399-iran-may-exit-ceasefire-over-israeli-attacks-on-lebanon---reports] the ceasefire agreement.

Limited ship movement resumed through the strait earlier after President Trump announced a temporary ceasefire agreement, and Fars said two tankers were granted permission to pass safely through the strait.

Lebanon's Health Ministry said Israeli airstrikes Wednesday killed dozens of people and injured hundreds more, with rescue efforts underway to find others still trapped under rubble.

Israel's military said it carried out strikes aimed at Hezbollah hideouts, some of them in densely populated areas in Beirut; Israel said militants are moving from their traditional stronghold in the city's southern suburbs toward civilian areas farther north.

Even before the Fars report, Hormuz appeared to remain largely blocked, with shipowners trying to understand if they can safely transit the vital waterway.

Amid the uncertainty and confusion [https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/08/trump-iran-ceasefire-strait-of-hormuz-toll.html], energy-related shipping shares trade mostly higher, including DHT Holdings (DHT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DHT]) up 3.5%, Frontline (FRO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FRO]) up 3.3%, Dorian LPG (LPG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LPG]) up 3.3%, BW LPG (BWLP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BWLP]) up 3.1%, CMB.Tech (CMBT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CMBT]) up 3%, International Seaways (INSW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/INSW]) up 2.9%, Scorpio Tankers (STNG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/STNG]) up 2.1%, Teekay (TK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TK]) up 2%, Tsakos Energy Navigation (TEN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TEN]) up 1.4%, Torm (TRMD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TRMD]) up 1.3%, Teekay Tankers (TNK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TNK]) up 1%, Okeanis Eco Tankers (ECO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ECO]) up 0.9%, Ardmore Shipping (ASC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ASC]) up 0.7%, Flex LNG (FLNG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FLNG]) up 0.4%.

"Should transits (to some degree) be possible in the coming two weeks, we might see a prioritization of emptying the Persian Gulf of its trapped tankers, before taking further vessels inside for new loadings," Fearnley analyst Fredrik Dybwad said [https://www.tradewindsnews.com/finance/shipping-stocks-fall-sharply-after-us-iran-ceasefire/2-1-1970598] in a note.

"Hence, this emerges as a risk for tanker rates should the trapped vessels exit and fighting escalate after a two-week period—as 10% of the compliant tanker fleet currently is trapped inside the strait," Dybwad wrote. "However, as a massive amount of barrels lie at port-side inventories inside the Gulf, a future scramble for tonnage in the case of a partial (or full) reopening of the Hormuz Strait will likely be positive for tanker rates... though we acknowledge this hinges on the strait not ‘closing’ after a short period of being open for business."

ETFs: (BWET [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BWET]), (BOAT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BOAT])

title: BW LPG Limited - 2025 Integrated Annual Report
content:
MAPLETREE BUSINESS CITY, SG / ACCESS Newswire / March 31, 2026 / BW LPG Limited ("BW LPG" or the "Company", OSE ticker code: "BWLPG.OL", NYSE ticker code "BWLP")(OSL:BWLPG)(AMEX:BWLP) today publishes its 2025 Integrated Annual Report and files its 2025 Annual Report on Form 20-F with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission ("SEC").

The 2025 Annual Report on Form 20-F includes the Company's audited consolidated financial statement for the year ended 31 December 2025.

Our 2025 Integrated Annual Report which incorporates our sustainability reporting is available on the Company's website: https://www.bwlpg.com/investor/financial-reports-presentations/

In accordance with Section 203.01 of the New York Stock Exchange Listed Company Manual, BW LPG confirms that the Company has filed its 2025 Annual Report on Form 20-F with the SEC.

The 2025 Annual Report on Form 20-F is available on the Company's website https://bit.ly/2025-Form-20-F and is also available on the SEC's website at www.sec.gov.

Shareholders may request a printed copy of the 2025 Annual Report on Form 20-F free of charge by email to investor.relations@bwlpg.com.

For further information, please contact:

Samantha Xu 
Chief Financial Officer
E-mail: investor.relations@bwlpg.com

About BW LPG

BW LPG is the world's leading owner and operator of LPG vessels, with a fleet of about 50 Very Large Gas Carriers (VLGCs), including 22 vessels powered by LPG dual-fuel propulsion technology. Building on over five decades of LPG shipping experience, the company is strengthened by an in-house LPG trading division and the commercial expertise to explore investments in value chain assets. Together, these capabilities enable BW LPG to provide trusted and reliable services for sourcing and delivering LPG to customers worldwide. 
Delivering energy for a better world - more information about BW LPG can be found at www.bwlpg.com.

BW LPG is associated with BW Group, a leading global maritime company involved in shipping, floating infrastructure, deepwater oil & gas production, and new sustainable technologies. Founded in 1955 by Sir YK Pao, BW controls a fleet of over 450 vessels transporting oil, gas and dry commodities, with its 200 LNG and LPG ships constituting the largest gas fleet in the world. In the renewables space, the group has investments in solar, wind, batteries, and water treatment.

This information is subject to disclosure requirements pursuant to Section 5-12 of the Norwegian Securities Trading Act.

Story Continues

BWG

Simply Wall St Dividend Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: BW LPG Limited is an investment holding company that operates in ship owning and chartering activities globally, with a market capitalization of NOK25.06 billion.

Operations: BW LPG Limited generates its revenue primarily through its global ship owning and chartering operations.

Dividend Yield: 8.6%

BW LPG Limited recently declared a cash dividend of US$0.57 per share for Q4 2025, aligning with its top-tier dividend yield of 8.65% in the Norwegian market. However, the sustainability of this dividend is questionable due to a high payout ratio of 91.8%, indicating dividends are not well covered by earnings despite being supported by cash flows with a cash payout ratio of 57.6%. Earnings have been volatile and are forecasted to decline, raising concerns about future dividend reliability.

Dive into the specifics of BWG here with our thorough dividend report. Our comprehensive valuation report raises the possibility that BWG is priced lower than what may be justified by its financials.OB:BWLPG Dividend History as at Mar 2026

CCID Consulting

Simply Wall St Dividend Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: CCID Consulting Company Limited, along with its subsidiaries, offers consulting services in the People’s Republic of China and has a market cap of HK$840 million.

Operations: CCID Consulting Company Limited generates revenue through its Data Platform Services (CN¥35.14 million), Decision-making Consulting Services (CN¥192.08 million), and Science and Technology Innovation Platform Services (CN¥88.38 million) in the People's Republic of China.

Dividend Yield: 9.9%

Story Continues

CCID Consulting's dividend yield of 9.88% ranks in the top 25% in Hong Kong, yet its sustainability is questionable. The high payout ratio of 95.2% suggests dividends are not well covered by earnings, though cash flows do cover them at an 88% cash payout ratio. Despite a decade of growth, dividend payments have been volatile and unreliable, with financial reports over six months old further complicating assessment of future stability.

Click here to discover the nuances of CCID Consulting with our detailed analytical dividend report. The analysis detailed in our CCID Consulting valuation report hints at an deflated share price compared to its estimated value.SEHK:2176 Dividend History as at Mar 2026

Cashbox Partyworld

Simply Wall St Dividend Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: Cashbox Partyworld Co., Ltd. and its subsidiaries provide audio-visual equipment for singing in Taiwan, with a market capitalization of NT$9.88 billion.

Operations: Cashbox Partyworld Co., Ltd.'s revenue segments include NT$3.55 billion from singing audio-visual equipment, NT$1.05 million from crop cultivation, and NT$12.98 million from food manufacturing and wholesale.

Dividend Yield: 8.4%

Cashbox Partyworld offers a dividend yield of 8.4%, placing it in the top 25% of Taiwan's market, though sustainability is a concern with an earnings payout ratio of 113.5%. Despite some growth over the past decade, dividends have been volatile and unreliable. While cash flows cover dividends with a payout ratio of 67.3%, recent financial data is outdated, complicating stability assessments. The stock trades at 28.4% below its estimated fair value, offering potential value for investors seeking high yields amidst risk factors.
